content:
## MARKET OVERVIEW

**1. What is the Current Market Regime?**
NEUTRAL — Normal volatility with very narrow participation. Vol: 26.6%. Breadth: 0/8 sectors positive.

**2. Which sectors are leading?**
Energy & Chemicals is the relative leader despite the pullback: TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND; PETKM (1W: +6.42% | 1M: +3.08%) ABOVE TREND. Heavy Industry shows isolated strength via ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND.

**3. Which sectors are lagging?**
Banking is the weakest by far: AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND; ISCTR (1W: -15.91% | 1M: -14.23%) BELOW TREND. Technology & Telecom and Transportation also lag materially (TTKOM and TAVHL among the worst).

## STOCK PERFORMANCE

**4. Top Performing Stocks?**
TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND; PETKM (1W: +6.42% | 1M: +3.08%) ABOVE TREND; ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND; TKFEN (1W: +2.57% | 1M: -9.79%) RECOVERY; CCOLA (1W: -1.12% | 1M: -4.28%) BELOW TREND.

**5. Worst Performing Stocks?**
ENKAI (1W: -11.90% | 1M: -6.76%) BELOW TREND; TTKOM (1W: -13.02% | 1M: -12.64%) BELOW TREND; TRMET (1W: -13.21% | 1M: +12.87%) CORRECTION; EREGL (1W: -13.66% | 1M: -1.74%) BELOW TREND; TRALT (1W: -15.08% | 1M: +12.41%) CORRECTION.

**6. Momentum Stocks?**
Strongest upside momentum: TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND and PETKM (1W: +6.42% | 1M: +3.08%) ABOVE TREND. Strongest downside momentum: YKBNK (1W: -18.34% | 1M: -7.58%) BELOW TREND and AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND.

## SECTOR OUTLOOK

**7. Banking Sector Outlook?**
Banking is under severe pressure and likely to remain volatile; AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND, GARAN (1W: -15.37% | 1M: -12.18%) BELOW TREND, ISCTR (1W: -15.91% | 1M: -14.23%) BELOW TREND. Avoid incremental exposure until credit/capital headlines or valuation-driven rebounds emerge.

**8. Industrial Sector Outlook?**
Mixed — defense and select heavy industry outperform while autos and steel are weak; ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND, EREGL (1W: -13.66% | 1M: -1.74%) BELOW TREND, FROTO (1W: -6.38% | 1M: -5.82%) BELOW TREND. Favor selective names with confirmed earnings/cashflow resilience.

**9. Energy & Chemicals Outlook?**
Energy shows the week’s biggest dispersion: TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND and PETKM (1W: +6.42% | 1M: +3.08%) ABOVE TREND, while TRALT (1W: -15.08% | 1M: +12.41%) CORRECTION and TRMET (1W: -13.21% | 1M: +12.87%) CORRECTION trade as profit-taking names. Price action will track oil, domestic demand signals and refining margins.

## PORTFOLIO STRATEGY

**10. Portfolio Recommendation?**
Neutral tactical stance: trim banks and broad cyclicals, lock gains in short-term winners (TUPRS/ASELS) and maintain cash for overt weakness; use short-dated hedges if index downside accelerates. Add selectively to fundamentally strong names on confirmed washouts or catalyst-driven rebounds.

## NEXT WEEK OUTLOOK

**11. Stocks to Watch Next Week?**
TUPRS (1W: +18.08% | 1M: +13.91%) ABOVE TREND for earnings/margin headlines; ASELS (1W: +3.57% | 1M: +17.02%) ABOVE TREND for momentum follow-through; AKBNK (1W: -17.86% | 1M: -12.26%) BELOW TREND and YKBNK (1W: -18.34% | 1M: -7.58%) BELOW TREND as bounce/rebalancing candidates; PETKM (1W: +6.42% | 1M: +3.08%) ABOVE TREND for sector flows.

**12. Sectors to Watch Next Week?**
Energy & Chemicals for margin and oil-driven volatility; Banking for downside continuation or oversold rebounds. Monitor Heavy Industry for idiosyncratic momentum names like ASELS.

**13. Key Levels & Triggers?**
Watch XU100 reaction around the recent 1W lows and the 1M range — a decisive break below the 1W lows would reinforce the current risk-off tilt while a reclaim of the 1M mid-range would signal stabilization. Key catalysts: CBRT communications, bank-specific news/earnings, oil price moves and major FX flows.

## EXECUTIVE SUMMARY
Neutral regime: normal volatility but zero sector breadth — market leadership narrow and risk concentrated. TUPRS and ASELS are the clear tactical longs; banks are the most damaged sector and merit reduced exposure until clearer catalysts or technical stabilization.
